cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about which statement accurately describes Yves Gomes based on the provided text. First, I'll go through each option one by one and check against the information given. Option A: "He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally." That suggests he was born in India and then came to the U.S. when he was very young. However, the parents were deported more than a year ago, and he was left in the U.S. But the question is about whether he was born in India. The text does not explicitly say he was born there, but it does say he left India at 14 months old, which implies he was born there. However, the parents were the ones who came to America, and he was with them from 14 months. So A might be correct, but I need to check other options. Option B: "His parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago." Wait, the text says that he was deported to India with his parents when he was 14 months old, but they were deported more than a year ago. Wait, no. Let me re-read the relevant part. The text says: "He is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old. They were deported more than a year ago." So he left India when he was 14 months old, but his parents were deported more than a year ago. Wait, that seems conflicting. Let me parse this carefully. The sentence is: "He is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old. They were deported more than a year ago." So he was taken to the U.S. when he was 14 months old (leaving India), and then his parents were deported more than a year ago (so some time after he was 14 months old). Therefore, the parents were deported more than a year ago, not that they left India. So option B is incorrect because it says "His parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago." But they were deported to India, not from India. So B is wrong. Option C: "He wants to finish school in America and become a nurse in India after graduation." The text says he wants to finish school and become a doctor. Also, he wants to become a U.S. citizen. So he's not planning to go back to India to be a nurse. Therefore, option C is incorrect. Option D: "He is optimistic about the passage of the DREAMAct." The text states that Yves Gomes hopes the DREAMAct will pass because he wants to finish school and become a citizen. The last sentence says, "He wants to finish school and eventually become a US citizen." The DREAMAct is his hope. The text also mentions that the DREAMAct has not passed yet and time is running out, but he hopes it won't be blocked. So he is optimistic about its passage. So D is correct.
